name: Grace Williams
technical_background: No coding experience. Strong strategic and analytical skills from 20 years in marketing leadership. Comfortable interpreting analytics dashboards and financial reports but not hands-on technical. Oversees vendors implementing personalization, ad-tech optimization, and marketing automation.

current_profile.summary: VP of Marketing with 20+ years of experience leading marketing organizations at major retail and e-commerce companies. Currently oversees a 25-person marketing team with a $12M annual budget covering brand, digital, lifecycle, and performance marketing. Strong strategy and leadership skills — regularly presents to the C-suite and board on marketing ROI, customer acquisition cost, and lifetime value. Comfortable interpreting analytics dashboards and directing data-driven campaigns but not hands-on technical. Uses ChatGPT for brainstorming campaign concepts, reviewing draft copy, and competitive analysis. Has explored Jasper AI for marketing content generation. Oversees vendors implementing personalization engines, ad-tech optimization, and marketing automation platforms. Understands AI value at a conceptual level but needs structured knowledge to lead AI investments, evaluate vendor claims, and align AI projects with brand, ROI, and compliance requirements.
technical_skills: Budget management and ROI analysis ($12M portfolio)
current_profile.ai_experience: Uses ChatGPT for brainstorming campaign concepts, reviewing copy drafts, and competitive intelligence. Has explored Jasper AI for content generation and evaluated its output quality. Oversees vendor-implemented AI features (product recommendation engines, dynamic pricing, personalized email content) but does not understand how the models work, how to evaluate their quality, or how to govern their use. Attended two AI in marketing conferences. Comfortable as a strategic buyer of AI capabilities but not as a builder or evaluator.

learning_intent: Wants executive-level AI literacy to lead AI investments strategically, ask better questions of vendors and internal teams, and align AI projects with brand integrity, customer experience, ROI, and compliance. Goal: develop frameworks for evaluating AI vendor proposals, set governance guardrails for marketing AI, understand personalization and content generation capabilities and limitations, and coach the marketing team on AI-augmented workflows. Not seeking to become technical — seeking to become an informed, effective AI leader.

target_jd.title: AI-Savvy Marketing Leader (Strategy + Governance)
requirements: Set marketing AI strategy: personalization, attribution modeling, content automation, and measurement optimization
Evaluate vendors and internal AI proposals; define success metrics, guardrails, and ROI expectations
Ensure privacy and responsible use in targeting, content generation, and customer data handling
Coach marketing teams on AI-augmented workflows and lead change management for AI adoption
Define governance frameworks for AI-generated marketing content (brand safety, accuracy, IP)
Align AI initiatives with customer experience strategy and brand values
Build business cases and budget proposals for AI marketing investments
Monitor AI feature performance and ROI; establish dashboards for marketing AI effectiveness
Partner with legal and compliance on data privacy requirements for AI-driven personalization
Develop AI literacy roadmap for the marketing organization

expected_skill_gaps.description: Practical understanding of LLM capabilities and limitations, data privacy, IP/copyright for content generation, and bias in marketing contexts.
AI product thinking: use-case selection and prioritization, workflow mapping for AI-enabled marketing operations, and feedback loop design.
AI governance fundamentals, risk framing for marketing AI, policy-to-controls for customer data, and regulations landscape overview.
Evaluating new AI tools systematically, building repeatable personal and team AI workflows, and teaching AI skills to the marketing organization.
Marketing-specific AI ethics: content generation ethics, brand safety in AI-generated content, and responsible targeting practices.
Understanding AI evaluation at a strategic level: why it matters, basic evaluation types, and what to look for in vendor quality claims.
AI security awareness for customer-facing marketing AI: data leakage risks and content safety considerations.
Writing AI project proposals for board approval and managing AI expectations across the organization.
